PART 1 — After Aiswarya finishes the architecture
"Thanks Aiswarya. So now let me show you how this actually works live — end to end."

"What I have here is a real campaign brief — the Banking Cloud Security campaign. 8 sections. This is the kind of brief that gets generated by our AI agent and goes to the marketing team. Before it goes anywhere, it needs to pass compliance."

"I'm going to fire it through the compliance evaluator right now and walk you through exactly what happens at each step."

PART 2 — Show the input
Open input.json in VS Code or Swagger. Scroll through it briefly.

"This is the input. A JSON with 8 sections — executive overview, value proposition, KPI framework, sales alignment, and so on. Each section has text content. And inside some of that content — there are intentional issues. Wrong brand casing, jargon, abbreviations, tone problems. Let me show you one."

Point at executive_summary:

"Here — 'NTT Data is a leader in digital services.' Two problems. One — wrong casing, should be NTT DATA. Two — we're opening with ourselves, not with the client's problem. That's a brand and framing violation. Let's see what the evaluator does with it."

PART 3 — Fire the API
Open Swagger at http://127.0.0.1:8001/docs. Go to POST /api/v1/compliance/evaluation-plan. Paste the input. Hit Execute.

"I'm calling the API now. Watch the terminal on the right — you'll see exactly what's happening step by step."

PART 4 — Walk through the terminal logs live
As the API runs, point at each step in the terminal:

Step 1:
"Step 1 — it's loading all the compliance rules from Cosmos DB. 833 rules. These come from the NTT DATA style guide — processed and stored as structured rules. Not hardcoded anywhere. Loaded fresh every time."

Step 2:
"Step 2 — contextual evaluation. This is Ipshita's piece. Azure OpenAI is now reading each section through 5 different lenses — brand, tone, framing, citations, sentence quality. For 8 sections that's 40 LLM calls, all firing concurrently. This is the deep check — understanding meaning, not just patterns."

Step 3:
"Step 3 — deterministic evaluation. This is Lakshmanan's piece. Pattern-based rules running on top of what the LLM already fixed. Regex, terminology checks, abbreviation rules, jargon rules. Fast, precise, no AI needed."

Step 4:
"Step 4 — rebuilding the corrected brief. All fixes from both evaluators applied to the text."

Step 5:
"Step 5 — saving the compliance run log to Cosmos. Every finding, every fix, timestamp, duration — all stored for audit."

Step 6:
"Step 6 — telemetry to App Insights. Latency, token usage, finding counts. So we can monitor this over time."

PART 5 — Show the output and findings table
Response comes back. Open demo_findings_table.txt.

"Right — the API returned the corrected brief. Now let me show you what changed."

"This is our findings table. Two sections — deterministic findings and contextual findings."

Walk through deterministic:

"On the deterministic side — 16 corrections in this one section alone. 'NTT Data' corrected to 'NTT DATA'. 'services & solutions' corrected to 'services and solutions' — ampersand not allowed in prose. '24/7' corrected to '24x7'. 'nextgen' corrected to 'next-generation technology'. 'learnings' corrected to 'lessons' — NTT DATA style guide says learnings is not approved vocabulary. These are all instant, automatic, no human needed."

Walk through contextual:

"On the contextual side — 3 findings from the LLM. First — the opening sentence. 'NTT Data is a leader' — flagged for NTT DATA-centric framing. The LLM rewrote it to lead with the banking challenge instead — 'Banking leaders need to modernize securely without increasing risk.' That's a tone and brand framing fix. Second — 'Tsuzumi 24x7 360 view' — flagged because Tsuzumi is used without any context. The LLM expanded it to a proper product description. Third — same framing issue on the opening — caught from the framing prompt lens."

PART 6 — Show the Cosmos run log
Open Azure Portal → Cosmos DB → db_mcc_compliance → compliance_run_logs → open the latest document.

"And this — this is the audit trail. Every time the API runs, one document is created here. Let me show you what's inside."

Scroll to findings array. Pick one item.

"This is one finding. Section ID — which section it came from. Rule ID — which specific rule triggered it. Issue type — error, warning, or suggestion. Evaluator type — deterministic or contextual. Flagged text — the exact phrase that was wrong. And the suggested fix. Everything is here."

Scroll to fixes array.

"And here are the fixes that were actually applied. Section, rule, original text, corrected text. So if anyone asks in 6 months — why does this brief say 'NTT DATA' and not 'NTT Data' — we open this document and the answer is here. Full traceability."

"Duration was X milliseconds for the full run. All 8 sections, both evaluators, 40 LLM calls."

CLOSING LINE
"So that's end to end. Brief goes in — both evaluators run — corrected brief comes out — full audit log saved. The key thing is — the rules are reusable. This same evaluator works for any brief, any campaign, any section. If the style guide changes — we update Cosmos, not the code."

"Ipshita, do you want to walk them through the contextual side in more detail?"
